subsections.name: Radio Frequency Testing
subsections.summary: Radio Frequency testing for UAV systems

entries.topic: Taiwan CAA map-software geofencing / warning requirements
entries.brief_summary: ETC EMC deck reproduces Article 12-style map-software requirements: drones with navigation equipment must have active restriction or warning functions to prevent entry into prohibited or restricted airspace and airport buffer zones, and designers / manufacturers must keep map information accurate and updated.

entries.topic: NCC RF type-approval framework for low-power and telecom radio devices
entries.brief_summary: ETC RF deck maps Taiwan NCC RF regulation families used for wireless products, including LP0002 for low-power radio-frequency devices, RTTE01 for 2.4 GHz radio-frequency telecommunications terminal equipment, and IS2031-0 for point-to-point microwave radio-frequency equipment.

emc.title: Electromagnetic Compatibility (EMC)
emc.description: EMC-related regulations, testing, certification, and export topics.

regulations.title: Regulations
regulations.description: Overview of EMC-related rules and standards.

testing.title: Testing
testing.description: Overview of EMC testing requirements and procedures.

subsections.name: EMC Testing
subsections.summary: Electromagnetic compatibility and immunity testing for UAV systems

entries.brief_summary: ETC EMC deck lists CNS 14676-2 (IEC 61000-4-2) as the ESD test method and, for under-2 kg drones, shows ESD performance criteria of contact discharge 4 kV and air discharge 8 kV.

entries.topic: Taiwan radiated immunity / radiated susceptibility requirements for drones
entries.brief_summary: ETC EMC deck maps radiated immunity to CNS 14676-3 (IEC 61000-4-3). For under-2 kg drones it shows 80 MHz-1 GHz at 10 V/m and 1.4-6.0 GHz at 3 V/m with 80% AM; for 2-25 kg drones it describes propeller-off, motor-speed stability testing in an anechoic chamber with a 10% fluctuation limit and no loss of function.

entries.topic: Taiwan ESD setup and laboratory conditions
entries.brief_summary: ETC EMC deck details ESD setup verification and laboratory conditions for IEC 61000-4-2 testing, including shielding room conditions, 15-35 C temperature, 30-60% humidity, 150 pF / 330 ohm generator parameters, and verification of the generator, return cable, bleeder resistors, and ground reference plane.

entries.topic: Conducted disturbance immunity (CS) requirements for Taiwan drone testing
entries.brief_summary: ETC EMC deck lists CNS 14676-6 (IEC 61000-4-6) for conducted disturbance immunity from 0.15 MHz to 80 MHz and shows an ability level of 10 V with 80% AM at 1 kHz.

entries.topic: Additional EFT, surge, magnetic-field, and voltage-dip immunity families in Taiwan drone testing
entries.brief_summary: ETC EMC deck adds Taiwan drone immunity families beyond ESD and RS: CNS 14676-4 / IEC 61000-4-4 for EFT, CNS 14676-5 / IEC 61000-4-5 for surge, CNS 14676-8 / IEC 61000-4-8 for magnetic-field immunity, and IEC 61000-4-11 for voltage dips and interruptions, with under-2 kg drone performance criteria shown on the deck.

entries.topic: Taiwan BSMI drone inspection standards for EMC emissions and immunity
entries.brief_summary: ETC EMC deck states that for drones under 2 kg, Taiwan BSMI inspection references CNS 15598-1 (IEC 62368-1 safety), CNS 15936 (CISPR 32 emissions), CNS 16197 (CISPR 35 immunity), CNS 14674-2 (IEC 61000-6-2 industrial immunity), and CAA map-software requirements under the drone management rules.

entries.topic: Taiwan drone immunity framework using CNS / IEC standards
entries.brief_summary: ETC EMC deck shows Taiwan drone immunity testing tied to CNS 16197 (CISPR 35), CNS 14674-2 (IEC 61000-6-2), and the specific IEC 61000-4-x immunity families, making Taiwan much more explicit on immunity than the earlier workbook version suggested.

entries.topic: EMC structure and EMI definitions used in Taiwan drone testing
entries.brief_summary: ETC EMC deck defines EMC as EMI plus EMS and explicitly separates conducted emission, radiated emission, conducted susceptibility, and radiated susceptibility; it frames EMI as electromagnetic energy emitted through radiation or conduction that interferes with other equipment.

entries.source_title: Taiwan’s Thunder Tiger drone added to US Blue UAS Cleared List
entries.brief_summary: Shows Taiwan firm entry onto the U.S. Blue UAS Cleared List after security, supply-chain-integrity, and performance vetting; also mentions anti-jamming GPS.
National defense report describing cyber threats, AI-enabled attacks, and defense resilience priorities; contextual support for Taiwan cyber-related certification needs.

entries.topic: MODA/ETC cyber test scope for UAVs
entries.source_title: The Ministry of Digital Affairs Marks Establishing UAV Cybersecurity Laboratories by Private Sector for One Year
entries.brief_summary: MODA states that the UAV Cybersecurity Joint Testing Laboratory focuses on product system security, software security, communication security, and firmware security; ETC is one of the participating labs in the mechanism.
